_comment: Three-task canary subset of TerminalBench-2 used by the harness matrix (Phase A.0/A.1 calibration runs and pre-flight smokes). Companion to termbench-subset.json. Selection was made for category breadth, not predicted pass rate — see _exclusion_rules below for what is intentionally NOT in this list.
_exclusion_rules: No 'hard' tasks: with only 3 reps in the matrix the per-cell variance on hard tasks dominates the harness signal. Hard tasks belong in the larger 20-task subset (termbench-subset.json), not the canary.

No tasks already known broken under our own adapter pre-existing: a task that flat-fails for fizeau regardless of model is noise, not signal, in a comparative matrix. As of 2026-04-29 no exclusions were needed; if a task starts failing structurally for fizeau, drop it from this canary and replace it with another medium task in the same category.

Three tasks only: the canary's job is to exercise the full (harness x profile x rep) loop end-to-end on a budget. Adding tasks dilutes that purpose — for broader category coverage use the full subset.

Stratification: one easy smoke (proves the adapter is wired), one data-processing medium (file edits + reasoning), one software-engineering medium (multi-step shell). This matches the breadth axes called out in docs/research/harness-matrix-plan-2026-04-29.md Step 6.
selection_rule: Three-task canary for the harness matrix: one smoke + two medium tasks across two categories. See _exclusion_rules for what is deliberately omitted. Bumping version requires re-deriving the per-run cost cap (Step 5) on the new task set.

tasks.rationale: Canonical TB hello-world; smoke that the adapter is wired end-to-end before spending model tokens on the medium tasks.
File edits + reasoning over a date-windowed log; exercises read-edit-run loops on a non-trivial text-processing problem.
Multi-step shell + git remediation; the highest expected token burn in the canary, used as the cost-cap calibration task in Step 5 of the matrix plan.
